scenario: Local-first sync engine vs traditional client-server.
alex_initial_position: Local-first (Replicache, Electric SQL). Local DB as source.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: Local-first means reads are instant, writes sync async.

debate.speaker: sam
debate.argument: Sync conflicts are complex. Traditional simpler.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: Replicache handles sync + conflicts. Library-level.

debate.speaker: sam
debate.argument: Adoption is rare. Bet a startup on it?

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: For offline-first apps, justify. For online-first, skip.

context_dependent_answer: Offline-first apps: local-first. Online-first: traditional.
decision_framework: debate-226: judge the state management architecture debate in this scenario: Local-first sync engine vs traditional client-server.. Separate claims, evidence, counterexamples, operational consequences, migration cost, and the failure mode each side handles poorly.
what_juniors_miss: Juniors don't consider local-first. Senior insight: for offline-first apps, local-first is the right paradigm.

scenario: Compound vs slot for layout primitives (Card, Modal).
alex_initial_position: Compound. Card.Header, Card.Body, Card.Footer.
sam_initial_position: Slot via named children. <Card header={...} footer={...}>{body}</Card>.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: Compound reads like HTML structure.

debate.speaker: sam
debate.argument: Named slots are explicit about layout.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: Both work. Compound feels React-natural.

context_dependent_answer: Either; consistency matters more than choice.
decision_framework: debate-227: judge the component composition patterns debate in this scenario: Compound vs slot for layout primitives (Card, Modal).. Separate claims, evidence, counterexamples, operational consequences, migration cost, and the failure mode each side handles poorly.
what_juniors_miss: Juniors flat-prop everything. Senior insight: compound clarifies optional + ordered structure.

scenario: Form persistence: localStorage vs IndexedDB vs sessionStorage.
alex_initial_position: localStorage. Simple, sync.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: localStorage 1-5KB sufficient for forms.

debate.speaker: sam
debate.argument: Sync I/O on main thread. Janks UI.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: For 1KB writes, jank minimal.

debate.speaker: sam
debate.argument: For large forms (uploaded files, rich content), IDB.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: Granted. Small: localStorage. Large: IDB.

context_dependent_answer: <5KB form: localStorage. >5KB or files: IDB. Ephemeral: session.
decision_framework: debate-228: judge the form library choices debate in this scenario: Form persistence: localStorage vs IndexedDB vs sessionStorage.. Separate claims, evidence, counterexamples, operational consequences, migration cost, and the failure mode each side handles poorly.
what_juniors_miss: Juniors persist all in localStorage. Senior insight: IDB for serious persistence.

scenario: Polling vs WebSocket vs SSE for live updates.
context: Dashboard with live metrics, low update frequency (every minute).
alex_initial_position: Polling. Simple, no infra.
sam_initial_position: SSE. Better than polling, simpler than WS.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: For 1-minute updates, polling is fine. No persistent connection cost.

debate.speaker: sam
debate.argument: Polling wastes requests when nothing changes.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: At 1/min, 60 requests/hour. Negligible.

debate.speaker: sam
debate.argument: For higher frequency, SSE.

context_dependent_answer: <1/min: polling. 1-10/min: SSE. >10/min: WS.
decision_framework: debate-229: judge the data fetching strategy debate in this scenario: Polling vs WebSocket vs SSE for live updates.. Separate claims, evidence, counterexamples, operational consequences, migration cost, and the failure mode each side handles poorly.
what_juniors_miss: Juniors use WS for everything. Senior insight: polling is fine for low-frequency.

scenario: Design tokens versioning.
context: Tokens shared across products, need versioning.
alex_initial_position: Semver for tokens. Breaking changes major.
sam_initial_position: Single source, no versioning. Atomic update.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: Products adopt new tokens at their pace.

debate.speaker: sam
debate.argument: Token drift across products. Inconsistency.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: Monorepo: atomic. Multi-repo: versioned.

context_dependent_answer: Monorepo: atomic. Multi-repo: versioned.
decision_framework: debate-230: judge the styling approach wars debate in this scenario: Design tokens versioning.. Separate claims, evidence, counterexamples, operational consequences, migration cost, and the failure mode each side handles poorly.
what_juniors_miss: Juniors don't version. Senior insight: shared deps need versioning when consumers don't all update together.

scenario: Page transitions: shared element vs cross-fade.
alex_initial_position: Shared element where elements continue across pages.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: Shared element transitions feel native (mobile app feel).

debate.speaker: sam
debate.argument: Implementation complex. Cross-fade good enough.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: View Transitions API made shared element easy.

debate.speaker: sam
debate.argument: Granted. View Transitions: shared. Else: cross-fade.

context_dependent_answer: View Transitions support: shared element. Fallback: cross-fade.
decision_framework: debate-231: judge the animation library decisions debate in this scenario: Page transitions: shared element vs cross-fade.. Separate claims, evidence, counterexamples, operational consequences, migration cost, and the failure mode each side handles poorly.
what_juniors_miss: Juniors don't transition. Senior insight: page transitions communicate spatial relationships.

scenario: E2E in CI: cost vs coverage.
alex_initial_position: Prune to 20 critical. Add nightly full run.
sam_initial_position: Parallelize across machines. Keep all in PR.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: 30 min PR is too slow. Devs work around.

debate.speaker: sam
debate.argument: Sharding across 5 machines = 6 min. Still all.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: 5x infra cost.

debate.speaker: sam
debate.argument: Worth it for confidence.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: Critical path PR + nightly full = balance.

context_dependent_answer: PR: critical path. Nightly: full. Pre-release: full + perf.
decision_framework: debate-232: judge the testing philosophy debate in this scenario: E2E in CI: cost vs coverage.. Separate claims, evidence, counterexamples, operational consequences, migration cost, and the failure mode each side handles poorly.
what_juniors_miss: Juniors all-or-nothing. Senior insight: tiered runs balance speed and coverage.

scenario: verbatimModuleSyntax flag.
context: Mixing types and values in imports.
alex_initial_position: Enable. Forces 'import type' for clarity.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: verbatimModuleSyntax ensures type imports erase cleanly. Avoids bundler edge cases.

debate.speaker: sam
debate.argument: Adds friction to every import.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: Auto-import in IDE adds 'type'. No manual work.

debate.speaker: sam
debate.argument: Granted. Enable.

context_dependent_answer: New projects: enable verbatimModuleSyntax.
decision_framework: debate-233: judge the typescript strictness debate in this scenario: verbatimModuleSyntax flag.. Separate claims, evidence, counterexamples, operational consequences, migration cost, and the failure mode each side handles poorly.
what_juniors_miss: Juniors mix type/value imports. Senior insight: explicit is better for bundlers.

scenario: Bundle analyzer in CI vs local-only.
alex_initial_position: CI gate. Fail PR if bundle grows >5%.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: Without CI gate, bundle grows silently.

debate.speaker: sam
debate.argument: Strict gate frustrates legitimate features.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: Threshold + override flag for legitimate.

debate.speaker: sam
debate.argument: Granted. Soft warning gate.

context_dependent_answer: Soft CI gate with override + visible PR comments.
decision_framework: debate-234: judge the performance vs dx tradeoffs debate in this scenario: Bundle analyzer in CI vs local-only.. Separate claims, evidence, counterexamples, operational consequences, migration cost, and the failure mode each side handles poorly.
what_juniors_miss: Juniors don't track bundle. Senior insight: bundle bloat is silent. Track in CI.

scenario: Source maps in production.
alex_initial_position: Ship source maps separately, served only to error tracker.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: Sentry needs source maps for stack traces.

debate.speaker: sam
debate.argument: Public source maps expose code.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: Upload to Sentry, don't ship publicly.

debate.speaker: sam
debate.argument: Granted. Sentry has source maps, public doesn't.

context_dependent_answer: Build maps, upload to Sentry, exclude from public.
decision_framework: debate-235: judge the build tool choices debate in this scenario: Source maps in production.. Separate claims, evidence, counterexamples, operational consequences, migration cost, and the failure mode each side handles poorly.
what_juniors_miss: Juniors skip maps or ship them publicly. Senior insight: maps for diagnostics; not for public.

scenario: Theming via CSS variables vs theme prop with object.
alex_initial_position: CSS variables. Runtime themeable.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: CSS variables change without React re-render.

debate.speaker: sam
debate.argument: Theme object via context is type-safe.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: CSS variables can be typed via @theme.

debate.speaker: sam
debate.argument: Granted. CSS variables + types via @theme.

context_dependent_answer: CSS variables for theming. Types via Tailwind @theme.
decision_framework: debate-236: judge the component library strategy debate in this scenario: Theming via CSS variables vs theme prop with object.. Separate claims, evidence, counterexamples, operational consequences, migration cost, and the failure mode each side handles poorly.
what_juniors_miss: Juniors pass theme object. Senior insight: CSS variables ARE the theme. No React involvement needed.

scenario: Async error in event handler.
alex_initial_position: try/catch in handler.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: try/catch is local. Clear.

debate.speaker: sam
debate.argument: Every handler needs same boilerplate. HOF DRYs.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: For 3 cases, manual. For 30, HOF.

debate.speaker: sam
debate.argument: Agreed.

context_dependent_answer: Few async handlers: try/catch. Many: HOF.
decision_framework: debate-237: judge the error boundary strategy debate in this scenario: Async error in event handler.. Separate claims, evidence, counterexamples, operational consequences, migration cost, and the failure mode each side handles poorly.
what_juniors_miss: Juniors forget try/catch in handlers. Senior insight: unhandled promise rejection is silent.

scenario: Inline critical CSS vs external stylesheet.
alex_initial_position: Inline critical. Render without external request.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: Inline = 0 round trips for above-fold CSS. LCP win.

debate.speaker: sam
debate.argument: Inline grows HTML size. Mobile data cost.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: 5-10KB inline beats 1 round trip on slow networks.

debate.speaker: sam
debate.argument: Granted. Inline critical, external for rest.

context_dependent_answer: Critical CSS inline, rest external + preload.
decision_framework: debate-238: judge the code splitting philosophy debate in this scenario: Inline critical CSS vs external stylesheet.. Separate claims, evidence, counterexamples, operational consequences, migration cost, and the failure mode each side handles poorly.
what_juniors_miss: Juniors load all CSS upfront. Senior insight: critical CSS inlined = faster LCP.

scenario: Focus styles: outline only vs box-shadow vs ring.
alex_initial_position: Ring utility (box-shadow). Designable, layered.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: Outline can be styled away by mistake. Ring is intentional.

debate.speaker: sam
debate.argument: outline is the platform default. Don't override.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: For design system, custom ring matches brand.

debate.speaker: sam
debate.argument: Granted. Ring with sufficient contrast.

context_dependent_answer: Custom ring with contrast 3:1+.
decision_framework: debate-239: judge the accessibility tradeoffs debate in this scenario: Focus styles: outline only vs box-shadow vs ring.. Separate claims, evidence, counterexamples, operational consequences, migration cost, and the failure mode each side handles poorly.
what_juniors_miss: Juniors remove outline. Senior insight: focus visibility is non-negotiable.

scenario: Domain-driven design (DDD) on frontend.
alex_initial_position: DDD on frontend. Bounded contexts, aggregates.
sam_initial_position: Frontend doesn't need DDD. UI-driven structure.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: Complex domains benefit from explicit modeling.

debate.speaker: sam
debate.argument: DDD is backend-shaped. UI has different concerns.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: Subset of DDD - bounded contexts - applies. Each feature folder is a context.

debate.speaker: sam
debate.argument: That's feature folders. DDD-flavored naming.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: Granted. Feature folders + domain logic separation.

context_dependent_answer: Feature folders + domain logic separation. Skip ceremonial DDD.

code_examples.code:
src/
  features/
    billing/
      domain/         // pure logic
      api/            // adapters
      components/     // UI
      hooks/          // React glue
      index.ts        // public API
    onboarding/
      ...
  shared/
  app/

decision_framework: debate-240: judge the frontend architecture patterns debate in this scenario: Domain-driven design (DDD) on frontend.. Separate claims, evidence, counterexamples, operational consequences, migration cost, and the failure mode each side handles poorly.
what_juniors_miss: Juniors organize by tech (components, hooks). Senior insight: organize by feature/domain.

scenario: Optimistic delete with undo.
context: User deletes item; want undo within 5s.
alex_initial_position: Hide locally, debounced API delete with undo.
sam_initial_position: Immediate API call with restore endpoint.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: Debounced delete saves API round-trip if user undoes.

debate.speaker: sam
debate.argument: Restore endpoint is simpler architecture.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: Debounced is more responsive - undo is local until timer.

debate.speaker: sam
debate.argument: Granted. Debounced delete with toast.

context_dependent_answer: Local hide + debounced API + toast undo.
decision_framework: debate-241: judge the state management architecture debate in this scenario: Optimistic delete with undo.. Separate claims, evidence, counterexamples, operational consequences, migration cost, and the failure mode each side handles poorly.
what_juniors_miss: Juniors immediately delete. Senior insight: undo UX requires optimistic + delayed commit.

scenario: Conditional wrapping (sometimes wrap with link, sometimes not).
context: Component that may or may not be linked.
alex_initial_position: Conditional wrapper helper.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: Helper handles both with one component.

debate.speaker: sam
debate.argument: Two components clearer.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: For variant condition (1 prop), helper. For diverged behavior, separate.

context_dependent_answer: 1 prop variation: helper. Multi-axis: separate.
decision_framework: debate-242: judge the component composition patterns debate in this scenario: Conditional wrapping (sometimes wrap with link, sometimes not).. Separate claims, evidence, counterexamples, operational consequences, migration cost, and the failure mode each side handles poorly.
what_juniors_miss: Juniors duplicate. Senior insight: conditional wrap is a clean pattern.

scenario: Multi-step form: single form vs separate forms per step.
alex_initial_position: Single form spanning steps. State preserved.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: Single form: validate all on final submit.

debate.speaker: sam
debate.argument: Per-step submit shows progress, allows backend partial save.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: Single form with per-step API saves achieves both.

debate.speaker: sam
debate.argument: Agreed.

context_dependent_answer: Single RHF form + per-step API save.
decision_framework: debate-243: judge the form library choices debate in this scenario: Multi-step form: single form vs separate forms per step.. Separate claims, evidence, counterexamples, operational consequences, migration cost, and the failure mode each side handles poorly.
what_juniors_miss: Juniors separate forms per step. Senior insight: state spanning steps is one form.

scenario: Prefetch data on hover with React Query.
context: Link to detail page; prefetch on hover.
alex_initial_position: Prefetch on hover. Faster click-to-load.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: Hover = high intent. Prefetch valuable.

debate.speaker: sam
debate.argument: Hover without click = waste. Focus is stricter signal.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: Wasted prefetch is small. Faster perceived load is large.

debate.speaker: sam
debate.argument: Granted. Hover prefetch.

context_dependent_answer: Prefetch on hover (desktop) + viewport (mobile).
decision_framework: debate-244: judge the data fetching strategy debate in this scenario: Prefetch data on hover with React Query.. Separate claims, evidence, counterexamples, operational consequences, migration cost, and the failure mode each side handles poorly.
what_juniors_miss: Juniors don't prefetch. Senior insight: prefetch on intent for perceived perf.

scenario: Component-scoped CSS vs global utility.
alex_initial_position: Pick one and standardize. Mixed is confusing.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: Mixed approach = no clear convention. New code uses whatever.

debate.speaker: sam
debate.argument: Different problems benefit from different tools.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: Tailwind covers 95%. Component-scoped for 5% edge cases.

debate.speaker: sam
debate.argument: Agreed. Tailwind default + escape hatch.

context_dependent_answer: Tailwind default + CSS Modules for complex.
decision_framework: debate-245: judge the styling approach wars debate in this scenario: Component-scoped CSS vs global utility.. Separate claims, evidence, counterexamples, operational consequences, migration cost, and the failure mode each side handles poorly.
what_juniors_miss: Juniors fight conventions. Senior insight: pick a default, document escape hatches.

scenario: AutoAnimate (Vue inspired) vs Framer Motion layout.
alex_initial_position: AutoAnimate. Simple, just works.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: AutoAnimate is one line: useAutoAnimate hook. List re-orderings auto-animate.

debate.speaker: sam
debate.argument: Framer layout gives control over spring config.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: For simple lists, control unneeded.

debate.speaker: sam
debate.argument: Granted. AutoAnimate for simple, Framer for complex.

context_dependent_answer: Simple list: AutoAnimate. Complex layout: Framer Motion.
decision_framework: debate-246: judge the animation library decisions debate in this scenario: AutoAnimate (Vue inspired) vs Framer Motion layout.. Separate claims, evidence, counterexamples, operational consequences, migration cost, and the failure mode each side handles poorly.
what_juniors_miss: Juniors hand-roll. Senior insight: AutoAnimate solves 80% in one line.

scenario: AAA pattern (Arrange-Act-Assert) vs given/when/then.
alex_initial_position: AAA. Standard.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: AAA familiar across languages.

debate.speaker: sam
debate.argument: BDD reads as spec.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: Same structure, different names. Pick consistently.

context_dependent_answer: AAA or GWT - consistent within codebase.
decision_framework: debate-247: judge the testing philosophy debate in this scenario: AAA pattern (Arrange-Act-Assert) vs given/when/then.. Separate claims, evidence, counterexamples, operational consequences, migration cost, and the failure mode each side handles poorly.
what_juniors_miss: Juniors don't structure tests. Senior insight: AAA/GWT makes tests scannable.

scenario: Const assertions for tuple types vs explicit.
alex_initial_position: as const. Concise.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: as const captures literal types and tuple structure.

debate.speaker: sam
debate.argument: Explicit annotation documents intent.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: For hooks (useState pattern), as const universal.

context_dependent_answer: Hooks: as const. Library: annotation.
decision_framework: debate-248: judge the typescript strictness debate in this scenario: Const assertions for tuple types vs explicit.. Separate claims, evidence, counterexamples, operational consequences, migration cost, and the failure mode each side handles poorly.
what_juniors_miss: Juniors return arrays losing tuple types. Senior insight: as const preserves tuple structure.

scenario: Profiler overhead in production.
context: Want to profile but worry about overhead.
alex_initial_position: React.Profiler is light. Use in prod for hot paths.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: Profiler overhead is <1ms per render. Negligible.

debate.speaker: sam
debate.argument: For 1000s of components, 1ms compounds.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: Targeted - only wrap hot paths.

debate.speaker: sam
debate.argument: Or sample - 1% users get full profiling. Statistical insight without full cost.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: Agreed. Sampled prod profiling.

context_dependent_answer: Sample 1-5% with Profiler on hot paths.
decision_framework: debate-249: judge the performance vs dx tradeoffs debate in this scenario: Profiler overhead in production.. Separate claims, evidence, counterexamples, operational consequences, migration cost, and the failure mode each side handles poorly.
what_juniors_miss: Juniors profile only in dev. Senior insight: prod profile captures real user devices/networks.

scenario: Build cache: local vs remote.
alex_initial_position: Remote cache. Team shares build artifacts.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: Remote cache means 'CI hit, dev gets it'. Huge speedup.

debate.speaker: sam
debate.argument: Shared cache leaks build artifacts. Some code is sensitive.

debate.speaker: alex
debate.argument: Self-hosted remote cache addresses privacy.

debate.speaker: sam
debate.argument: Granted. Self-hosted remote cache.

context_dependent_answer: Self-hosted remote Turbo cache for team. Vercel-hosted for open projects.
decision_framework: debate-250: judge the build tool choices debate in this scenario: Build cache: local vs remote.. Separate claims, evidence, counterexamples, operational consequences, migration cost, and the failure mode each side handles poorly.
what_juniors_miss: Juniors rebuild from scratch. Senior insight: build cache is a team productivity multiplier.
